detaileddescription.para: This directory contains the core modules of the AnalysisG framework, a toolkit for data analysis that appears to be focused on graph-based models, possibly for particle physics data analysis.

sect1.title: Module Structure
sect1.para: The repository is organized into multiple specialized modules:

listitem.para: typecasting: Handles type conversion between different data representations
notification: Provides notification mechanisms in the framework
structs: Contains data structure definitions used throughout the framework
tools: General utility functions and tools
particle: Classes and functions for particle physics data representation
selection: Data selection and filtering functionality
model: Model definition and training functionality
metrics/metric: Evaluation metrics for model performance
lossfx: Loss functions for model training
optimizer: Optimization algorithms for model training

sect2.title: Model Module
sect2.para: The model module provides templates and implementations for machine learning models. The model_template class serves as a base class for implementing different models, with capabilities for:

listitem.para: Registering modules within the model architecture
Handling forward passes with graph-based data

sect2.title: IO Module
sect2.para: The IO module handles all input/output operations within the framework, including:

sect2.title: Structs Module
sect2.para: This module defines fundamental data structures used throughout the framework:

sect1.title: Integration
sect1.para: The modules are integrated through CMake build system, with interdependencies managed through header inclusions. The main CMakeLists.txt configures paths and builds all submodules in the correct order of dependencies.

sect2.title: model_template
sect2.para: Base class for model implementations with the following key methods:

listitem.para: register_module(): Adds neural network components to the model
forward(): Performs the forward pass for inference
assign_features(): Maps input data to appropriate tensors

sect2.title: io
sect2.para: Handles file operations and settings management:

listitem.para: import_settings(): Loads configuration from external sources

sect1.title: Development Guidelines
sect1.para:
When extending the framework:
Add new module directories to CMakeLists.txt
Ensure proper header inclusion
Maintain consistent naming conventions
Document public interfaces
Follow the existing patterns for object lifecycle management
